title: Type Operators, Mapped Types, Conditional Types, and Templates · Contract Defense
summary: Derive form, event, and patch contracts from one domain model without duplicating names or accepting impossible states. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a derived contract and event-type library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use type-context typeof to derive from runtime declarations without confusing it with runtime typeof.
Map over property keys with modifiers, key remapping, and value transforms while preserving intended optionality.
Use distributive and non-distributive conditional types with explicit true and false branch meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Typeof Type Context evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Typeof Type Context assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Derive public patch, readonly view, and event-name types from a service model with id, status, and retryCount. A newly added region field must flow through the intended transform without exposing internal secret fields. Competency probe: Use type-context typeof to derive from runtime declarations without confusing it with runtime typeof.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Typeof Type Context has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Typeof Type Context precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Type-context typeof executes the referenced value.
